key_insight: BatteryMat uses a three-tier ML-to-DFT screening pipeline to rapidly rank lithium-ion cathode candidates while preserving thermodynamic consistency through automated voltage validation.
engineering_relevance: It enables scalable cathode discovery from millions of structures by combining fast surrogate screening with higher-fidelity DFT checks before experimental investment.
method: ALIGNN screens JARVIS-DFT and Alexandria structures for candidate cathodes.
ALIGNN-FF predicts delithiation voltage profiles as an intermediate validation tier.
Automated PBE+U or optB88-vdW+U DFT recomputes average voltages with a consistent Li-metal reference.
evidence_signal: ALIGNN achieved MAE 0.17 V and R² = 0.94 against 7,610 ALIGNN-FF delithiation voltages, and the DFT tier matched experimental average voltages within about 0.3 V for four benchmark cathodes.
watch_item: The top-ranked structures are only surrogate-level leads until DFT validation, and the ALIGNN metric reflects fidelity to the force-field labels rather than direct agreement with experiment.
paper_title: BatteryMat: a hierarchical machine-learning and DFT framework for average-voltage screening of lithium-ion cathode materials
